perturbation.original_text: Neither the Franchisor, its Predecessor nor any person listed under Item 2 or an affiliate offering franchises under Franchisor's principal trademark: (A) has an administrative, criminal or civil action pending against that person alleging: a felony; a violation of a franchise, antitrust or securities law; fraud; embezzlement; fraudulent conversion; misappropriation of property; unfair or deceptive practices; or comparable civil or misdemeanor allegations.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither the Franchisor, its Predecessor nor any person listed under Item 2 or an affiliate offering franchises under Franchisor's principal trademark: (A) has an administrative, criminal or civil action pending against that person alleging: a felony; a violation of a franchise, antitrust or securities law; fraud; embezzlement; fraudulent conversion; misappropriation of property; unfair or deceptive practices; or comparable civil or misdemeanor allegations, unless the action is deemed frivolous by the Franchisor.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by allowing the Franchisor to subjectively deem legal actions as 'frivolous'. This contradicts the overall intent of the clause, which is to provide a clear and objective assessment of potential legal risks associated with the Franchisor and its affiliates. By adding this subjective clause, the Franchisor can potentially disregard or downplay legitimate legal actions, creating uncertainty and risk for the franchisee.
perturbation.contradicted_text: LITIGATION Neither the Franchisor, its Predecessor nor any person listed under Item 2 or an affiliate offering franchises under Franchisor's principal trademark: (A) has an administrative, criminal or civil action pending against that person alleging: a felony; a violation of a franchise, antitrust or securities law; fraud; embezzlement; fraudulent conversion; misappropriation of property; unfair or deceptive practices; or comparable civil or misdemeanor allegations, unless the action is deemed frivolous by the Franchisor. (B) has been convicted of a felony or pleaded nolo contendere to a felony charge or, within the ten year period immediately preceding the application for registration, has been convicted of or pleaded nolo contendere to a misdemeanor charge or has been the subject of a civil action alleging: violation of a franchise; anti-fraud or securities law; fraud; embezzlement; fraudulent conversion or misappropriation of property; unfair or deceptive practices; or comparable allegations. (C) is subject to a currently effective injunctive or restrictive order or decree relating to the franchise, or under a Federal, State or Canadian franchise, securities, antitrust, trade regulation or trade practice law, resulting from a concluded or pending action or proceeding brought by a public agency; or is subject to any currently effective order of any national securities association or national securities exchange, as defined in the Securities and Exchange Act of 1934, suspending or expelling such person from membership in such association or exchange; or is subject to a currently effective injunctive or restrictive order relating to any other business activity as a result of an action brought by a public agency or department, including, without limitation, actions affecting a license as a real estate broker or sales agent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause allowing the Franchisor to deem legal actions as 'frivolous,' which directly contradicts the original intent of the LITIGATION section to provide an objective assessment of legal risks. By introducing this subjective element, the changed text creates uncertainty and allows the Franchisor to potentially disregard legitimate legal actions, rendering the LITIGATION clause unreliable and therefore creating a strong in-text contradiction. The original clause intends to list all pending legal actions, but the changed text allows some to be hidden, breaking compatibility. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: BANKRUPTCY Neither the Franchisor, its affiliate, its predecessor, officers, or general partner during the ten year period immediately before the date of the disclosure document: (a) filed as debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code (or any comparable foreign law); (b) obtained a discharge of its debts under the bankruptcy code; or (c) was a principal officer of a company or a general partner in a partnership that either filed as a debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code or that obtained a discharge of its debts under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code during or within one year after the officer or general partner of the Franchisor held this position in the company or partnership.
perturbation.changed_text: BANKRUPTCY Neither the Franchisor, its affiliate, its predecessor, officers, or general partner during the ten year period immediately before the date of the disclosure document: (a) filed as debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code (or any comparable foreign law); (b) obtained a discharge of its debts under the bankruptcy code, unless such discharge was due to unforeseen circumstances deemed acceptable by the Franchisor; or (c) was a principal officer of a company or a general partner in a partnership that either filed as a debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code or that obtained a discharge of its debts under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code during or within one year after the officer or general partner of the Franchisor held this position in the company or partnership.
perturbation.explanation: The change in section 4(b) introduces an in-text contradiction by granting the Franchisor subjective discretion to deem certain bankruptcy discharges 'acceptable' due to 'unforeseen circumstances'. This contradicts the objective nature of the original clause, which seeks to provide a transparent and unbiased disclosure of bankruptcy history. The subjective assessment creates uncertainty, as the franchisee has no way to determine what circumstances the Franchisor might find 'acceptable', undermining the clause's purpose and creating a potential conflict of interest if the Franchisor downplays its own or its affiliates' bankruptcy history.
perturbation.contradicted_text: BANKRUPTCY Neither the Franchisor, its affiliate, its predecessor, officers, or general partner during the ten year period immediately before the date of the disclosure document: (a) filed as debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code (or any comparable foreign law); (b) obtained a discharge of its debts under the bankruptcy code, unless such discharge was due to unforeseen circumstances deemed acceptable by the Franchisor; or (c) was a principal officer of a company or a general partner in a partnership that either filed as a debtor (or had filed against it) a petition to start an action under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code or that obtained a discharge of its debts under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code during or within one year after the officer or general partner of the Franchisor held this position in the company or partnership.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a subjective element ('unless such discharge was due to unforeseen circumstances deemed acceptable by the Franchisor') into a clause that was previously objective. This directly contradicts the intent of the original clause, which aimed to provide a clear and unbiased record of bankruptcy history. The Contradicted Text, which is the same as the Changed Text, now contains this subjective exception, creating an in-text contradiction by allowing the Franchisor to potentially conceal or downplay bankruptcy events based on their own subjective assessment. This inconsistency regarding the disclosure of bankruptcy events creates a strong contradiction.
